questions.q: How many packets are exchanged in a normal TCP connection establishment?
What state is used to wait for late packets after closing a connection?
What is the purpose of window scaling?
questions.a: To allow TCP receive windows larger than 64 KB

questions.q: What is the default Linux congestion controller mentioned on the page?
Why does TIME_WAIT last for 2 x MSL?
questions.a: To let delayed duplicate segments expire before the same 4-tuple is reused.
It reduces latency by sending small writes immediately instead of coalescing them.

questions.q: Why does HTTP/3 move away from TCP?
questions.a: To avoid TCP head-of-line blocking and get stream-level recovery over QUIC.

questions.q: What does the listen() backlog control?
questions.a: The number of completed connections waiting in the accept queue.

questions.q: How do SYN cookies defend against SYN floods?
questions.a: They encode state in the initial sequence number so the server avoids allocating half-open connection state.
Each ACK confirms receipt of all bytes up to the acknowledged sequence number.

questions.q: Why does TCP use a 3-way handshake instead of 2 packets?
questions.a: So both sides can confirm reachability and agree on initial sequence numbers before data flows.

questions.q: Why is slow start called slow start even though cwnd grows exponentially?
questions.a: Because it begins with a tiny window and only ramps up quickly after connection start to avoid overwhelming the path.

questions.q: When is BBR preferable to loss-based congestion control?
questions.a: On high-bandwidth or long-RTT paths where keeping queues short matters more than reacting to packet loss.

questions.q: Why does TCP suffer from head-of-line blocking?
questions.a: The receiver must deliver bytes in order, so a missing segment blocks later data in the same stream.

questions.q: Why can a spoofed source IP not complete a TCP handshake easily?
questions.a: The attacker would need to receive the SYN-ACK and return the final ACK with the correct sequence state.

questions.q: What tradeoff does SYN cookie mode make during a flood?
questions.a: It preserves server resources but can reduce the amount of TCP option state the server remembers.
